heading: Use of foreign mission in manner incompatible with its status as foreign mission
label: 4315 Use of foreign mission in manner incompatible with its status as foreign mission
notes: Codification, referred to in subsec. (d), was in the original “the date of the enactment of this Act”, which was translated as meaning the date of enactment of , which enacted this section, to reflect the probable intent of Congress.December 22, 1987Pub. L. 100–204

Effective Date, , , provided that:Pub. L. 100–204, title I, § 128(b)101 Stat. 1343Dec. 22, 1987“(1)Except as provided in paragraph (2), the amendment made by subsection (a) [enacting this section] shall apply to any foreign mission beginning on the date of enactment of this Act [].Dec. 22, 1987“(2)(A)The amendment made by subsection (a) shall apply beginning 6 months after the date of enactment of this Act with respect to any nonimmigrant alien who is using a foreign mission as a residence or a place of business on the date of enactment of this Act.“(B)The Secretary of State may delay the effective date provided for in subparagraph (A) for not more than 6 months with respect to any nonimmigrant alien if the Secretary finds that a hardship to that alien would result from the implementation of subsection (a).”

children.heading: Establishment of limitation on certain uses
children.label: a Establishment of limitation on certain uses
children.text: A foreign mission may not allow an unaffiliated alien the use of any premise of that foreign mission which is inviolable under United States law (including any treaty) for any purpose which is incompatible with its status as a foreign mission, including use as a residence.

children.heading: Temporary lodging
children.label: b Temporary lodging
children.text: For the purposes of this section, the term “residence” does not include such temporary lodging as may be permitted under regulations issued by the Secretary.

children.heading: Waiver
children.label: c Waiver
children.text: The Secretary may waive subsection (a) with respect to all foreign missions of a country (and may revoke such a waiver) 30 days after providing written notification of such a waiver, together with the reasons for such waiver (or revocation of such a waiver), to the Committee on Foreign Affairs of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Foreign Relations of the Senate.

children.heading: Report
children.label: d Report
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Secretary of State shall submit a report to the Congress concerning the implementation of this section and shall submit such other reports to the Congress concerning changes in implementation as may be necessary.December 22, 1987
the term “foreign mission” includes any international organization as defined in ; andsection 4309(b) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: is admitted to the United States as a nonimmigrant, and

children.label: B
children.text: is not a member, or a family member of a member, of a foreign mission of that foreign country.
